data.title: Herc withdraws/refiles HSR for H&E Equipment deal
data.description:
Herc Holdings (NYSE:HRI) said it withdrew and refiled its HSR notification for its planned acquisition of H&E Equipment Services (NASDAQ:HEES). 

The refiling allows the Federal Trade Commission more time to complete is review of the deal, Herc said in a filing on Monday. 

"We remain fully committed to securing the necessary regulatory approvals to complete this transaction and are confident in the long-term value it will create for our combined team and all of our stakeholders," Herc CEO Larry Silber wrote.

Equipment rental supplier Herc (NYSE:HRI)  agreed to buy H&E Equipment (NASDAQ:HEES) in February.

data.title: IPO Roundup: Chagee Holdings, OMS Energy and more
data.description:
The U.S. IPO market remains staggered as volatility sparked by tariff-related news keeps the markets on edge. However, looking to calm investor nerves, tea-chain operator Chagee Holdings raising close to $400M as it plans its IPO on the U.S. markets. 

"The market opportunity for teahouses in China is large but features significant and fragmented competition from large chains as well as independent operators," said SA Investing Group Leader Donovan Jones in Chinese Teahouse Franchisor Chagee Begins U.S. IPO Effort. 

The firm currently intends to pay zero dividends in the near term and will retain future earnings for its growth and expansion plans, Jones added. 

Upcoming IPOs/SPACs that are expected to be priced this week

  • Everbright Digital Holdings (EDHL) is raising $7.0M by offering 1.5M shares at a price range of $4.00 and $5.00. Description: Marketing company.
  • J-Star Holding (YMAT) is raising $5.6M by offering 1.3M shares at a price range of $4.00 and $5.00. Description: Holding company with no material operations.
  • Kandal M. Venture Ltd. (FMFC) is raising $9.0M by offering 2.0M shares at a price range of $4.00 and $5.00. Description: Leather goods maker. 
  • OFA Group (OFAL) is raising $6.8M by offering 1.5M shares at a price of $4.50. Description: Architectural services company. 
  • Pinnacle Food Group (PFAI) is raising $8.1M by offering 1.8M shares at a price of $4.50. Description: Farming solutions company.
  • Robot Consulting Co. (LAWR) is raising $8.1M by offering 1.8M shares at a price of $4.50. Description: HRM software company. 
  • The Great Restaurant Development Holdings Limited (HPOT) is raising $7.0M by offering 1.4M shares at a price range of $4.00 and $6.00. Description: Chinese restaurant chain
  • Haoxin Holdings (HXHX) is raising $8.8M by offering 1.8M shares at a price range of $4.00 and $6.00. Description: Delivery services provider. 
  • Phoenix Asia Holdings (PHOE) is raising $8.0M by offering 1.8M shares at a price range of $4.00 and $6.00. Description: Holding company.
  • Jyong Biotech Ltd. (MENS) is raising $21.7M by offering 2.7M shares at a price range of $7.50 and $8.50. Description: Biotechnology company. 
  • OMS Energy Technologies (OMSE) is raising $50.0M by offering 5.6M shares at a price range of $8.00 and $10.00. Description: Surface wellhead systems maker.

Filing, terms, and changes

  • New Providence Acquisition III (NPACU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $261M in an initial public offering. Description: Consumer-focused SPAC.
  • Twelve Seas Investment III (OTC:TWLVU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $150M in an initial public offering. Description: SPAC.
  • Gores Holdings X (GTENU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $260M in an initial public offering. Description: SPAC.
  • Pelican Acquisition (PELIU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $75M in an initial public offering. Description: Technology-focused SPAC. 
  • AIRO Group Holdings (AIRO) has set terms with the SEC to raise up to $75M in an initial public offering. Description: Aerospace and defense company. 
  • Chagee Holdings (CHA) has set terms with the SEC to raise up to $396M in an initial public offering. Description: China-based teahouse chain. 
  • Dreamland (TDIC) has set terms with the SEC to raise up to $9M in an initial public offering. Description: Hong Kong-based event management company. 

Withdrawals

  • Pyro AI as withdrawn its plans for an initial public offering. Previously, the company had filed in 2024 with the SEC to raise about $10M.

Lock-up period expirations 

  • PTLE (PTLE) on 4/14/2025. Price movement over the past six months: -71.0%
  • Samfine Creation Holdings Group (SFHG) on 4/14/2025. Price movement over the past six months: -82.5%
  • Oriental Rise Holdings Limited (ORIS) on 4/15/2025. Price movement over the past six months: -37.3%

Since the start of the year, the Renaissance IPO ETF (NYSEARCA:IPO) has fallen about 18.1%, underperforming the S&P 500 index, which has lost about 8.8%.

data.title: Dr Reddy’s initiates 25% workforce reduction, report suggests
data.description:
Dr. Reddy's Laboratories (NYSE:RDY) will be laying off 25% of its workforce as the company moves to offset impacts from Revlimid-linked margin pressures, according to a report by Business Standard.

The India-based pharmaceutical major is already implementing the downsizing of its staff. A number of senior executives have been asked to resign, including many employees who earn over Rs 1 crore annually, Business Standard said, citing sources familiar with the matter.

The report also mentioned that staff in the 50-55 year age group working for the R&D division have been offered voluntary retirement.

The move, termed as "the internal directive to reduce manpower-related expenses", comes at a time when Dr. Reddy's is grappling with Revlimid revenue declines and needs to downsize teams in the nutraceuticals arm and the therapeutics division, with the latter expected to shut down, the  report suggested.

The company's U.S-listed stock is up about 1% premarket, while YTD, the stock has seen a 16.3% fall.

data.title: Momentus, Velo3D enter master services agreement
data.description:
Momentus (NASDAQ:MNTS) has entered into a five-year master services agreement with Velo3D (OTC:VLDX) to achieve faster, lower-cost production of space system components.

As part of the all-stock transaction, Momentus plans to leverage the advanced additive manufacturing capabilities of Velo3D to optimize spacecraft design with the potential for lighter, stronger, and more complex geometries; reduce production costs; and design satellite and space system components, manufactured by Velo3D, to supply to customers in the space industry.

In exchange for the services to be provided by Velo3D, Momentus issued 477,455 shares of Class A common stock and 673,408 shares of non-voting Series A Convertible Preferred Stock.

The deal also contains a provision, under which Velo3D may use capacity not utilized by Momentus to provide services to other customers. Momentus will be compensated for such use based on a formula equal to 20% of $3M less service fees attributed to the company in the first year and 50% of $3M less service fees attributed in each subsequent year of the agreement.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Freddie Mac sells non-performing loans worth ~$5.3M
data.description:
Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (OTCQB:FMCC) announced on Monday the sale of non-performing loans worth ~$5.3M.

Freddie Mac sold via auction 28 "deeply delinquent" non-performing residential first lien loans from its mortgage-related investments portfolio to VRMTG ACQ.

The transaction is expected to settle in June 2025.

The company said it had begun marketing the transaction on March 6 to potential bidders active in the NPL market.

data.title: Nvidia ticks up as company says it will manufacture and test chips in US
data.description:
Following the Trump administration's exemption for phones, semiconductors and computers from additional tariffs, Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) announced it would manufacture and test chips in the U.S.

Shares rose 3.7% in premarket trading.

In a post on its website, Nvidia said it had commissioned more than a million square feet of manufacturing space to build and test NVIDIA Blackwell chips in Arizona and AI supercomputers in Texas.

Additionally, Nvidia is working with Amkor Technology (AMKR) and SPIL for packaging and testing operations in Arizona.

“The engines of the world’s AI infrastructure are being built in the United States for the first time,” said Nvidia founder and CEO Jensen Huang. “Adding American manufacturing helps us better meet the incredible and growing demand for AI chips and supercomputers, strengthens our supply chain and boosts our resiliency.” 

Nvidia added that its Blackwell line of GPUs have already started production at Taiwan Semiconductor's (TSM) fabs in Phoenix. Additionally, it is working with manufacturing partner Foxconn to build supercomputer manufacturing plants in Houston, and with another partner, Wistron, in Dallas.

Mass production at both plants is expected to ramp up in the next 12 to 15 months, Nvidia added.

data.title: Choice Hotel's domestic-based demographic earns double upgrade at Goldman
data.description:
To address the shifting geopolitical and economic landscape, coupled with negative read-across from the U.S. airline industry, Goldman Sachs has lowered its outlook on certain names within the U.S. lodging and timeshare industry and raised its assessment of those asset-light stocks that enjoy more global diversity and are less indexed to international tourism into the U.S.

Against this backdrop, Goldman analysts Lizzie Dove and Ryan Davis have downgraded Hyatt (H) to Sell, Marriott (MAR) and Hilton (HLT) to Neutral, while simultaneously issuing a double upgrade to Choice Hotels (NYSE:CHH) to Buy from Sell.

Across the travel industry, lodging stocks have sold off materially with Hilton (HLT), Marriott (MAR), and Hyatt (H) all down by double digits over the past two months. The trend has been amplified by U.S. trade policy that Goldman views as already detrimental to inbound travel trends. Accordingly, Dove and Davis lowered their U.S. RevPAR estimates by ~125 basis points for 2025.

During the last shock to the travel industry, hotels that maintained an asset light, fee-based model, and unloaded owned and leased hotels, as well as timeshares outperformed the other C-corps during COVID.

“The pandemic was a great proof point of the resiliency of these business models, with U.S. RevPAR down ~80%, yet cash flow was fairly resilient,” said Dove and Davis.

As one of the most defensive names in Goldman’s coverage, Choice Hotels (NYSE:CHH) will likely be less “significantly” impacted by the current economic backdrop than the others as the majority of its customers originate within the U.S., shielding the company from declines in international tourism.

On the other end, Goldman considers Hyatt (H) one of its most macro-sensitive stocks among lodging, downgrading the stock to Sell from Neutral.

“While we appreciate Hyatt’s shift to asset light fees over the last few years, we are slightly more cautious versus peers due to Hyatt’s higher mix of management contracts, China exposure and more limited embedded growth in the in-construction pipeline.”

Goldman also downgraded Hilton (HLT) and Marriott (MAR), but with a business model that remains “best in class,” significant cash flow per share, and resilient balance sheets, both were downgraded to Neutral in respect of a valuation that is “still too high.”

Ahead of Monday’s open, shares of Choice Hotels (NYSE:CHH) are up more than 3%, while Hilton (HLT), Hyatt (H), and Marriott (MAR) are all marginally lower.

data.title: Morgan Stanley closes private equity fund at $2.3B in commitments
data.description:
Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) Investment Management  ("MSIM") reported on Monday  the final close of its North Haven Private Equity Co-Investment Opportunities Fund III (PECO III) oversubscribed at its hard cap with ~$2.3B in total commitments.

PECO III offers private equity exposure through co-investments alongside best-in-class buyout managers operating primarily in the lower middle market. The fund is the successor to two dedicated co-investment funds — PECO I and PECO II — and the continuation of Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) Private Equity Solutions' 25-year history of executing co-investments, including almost $6B of commitments since its inception at Morgan Stanly in 1999.

Morgan Stanley (MS) stock rose 1.8% in Monday premarket trading.

data.title: Air travel slumps as Apollo points to a noticeable decline in airports traffic
data.description:
Air travel has dropped notably across the ten busiest U.S. airports over the past two months, according to Apollo Asset Management, as both consumer sentiment and business travel weaken. Daily arrival data since late February points to a consistent and rapid decline in traffic.

“This is likely a mix of declines in business travel, tourism, and government travel,” Torsten Sløk, Apollo’s chief economist stated. 

See the below chart that Apollo Asset Management provided:

Expanding off Apollo Asset Management’s data point, Seeking Alpha constructed a list of the top 15 U.S. airline carriers according to its quant metrics. See the list below sorted from low to high.

No. 15: JetBlue Airways (NASDAQ:JBLU), quant rating of 1.80.

No. 14: Surf Air Mobility (NYSE:SRFM), quant rating of 2.55.

No. 13: Allegiant Travel Company (NASDAQ:ALGT), quant rating of 2.62.

No. 12: Frontier Group Holdings (NASDAQ:ULCC), quant rating of 2.73.

No. 11: Jet.Ai (NASDAQ:JTAI), quant rating of 2.83. 

No. 10: Sun Country Airlines (NASDAQ:SNCY), quant rating of 2.83.

No. 9: Alaska Air Group (NYSE:ALK), quant rating of 2.88. 

No. 8: Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL), quant rating of 2.94.

No. 7: Joby Aviation (NYSE:JOBY), quant rating of 2.99.

No. 6: American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL), quant rating of 3.09. 

No. 5: flyExclusive (NYSE:FLYX), quant rating of 3.15. 

No. 4: United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL), quant rating of 3.20. 

No. 3: Blade Air Mobility (NASDAQ:BLDE), quant rating of 3.30. 

No. 2: Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV), quant rating of 3.39. 

No. 1: SkyWest (NASDAQ:SKYW), quant rating of 4.28. 

For reference, Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics. 

Three airline exchange traded funds that may be worth analyzing in further detail are the U.S. Global Jets ETF (NYSEARCA:JETS), MAX Airlines 3X Leveraged ETN (JETU), and the MAX Airlines -3X Inverse Leveraged ETN (JETD).

data.title: UBS sees a tough road ahead for Stellantis
data.description:
UBS lowered its rating on Stellantis N.V. (NYSE:STLA) to Neutral after having the automobile stock slotted with a Buy rating. 

Analyst Patrick Hummel noted that the 25% U.S. auto tariffs introduced on April 2 hit Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) harder than Detroit peers because ~35% of the company's vehicles sold in the US are imported. He also sees Stellantis (STLA) having a harder time regaining market share in a likely shrinking U.S. market and warns of a high probability of losses in North America and negative free cash flow. 

"Without the perspective of a successful US turnaround, a core element to our Buy thesis no longer  exists. After sharp recent underperformance, we think STLA shares already discount this outcome," highlighted Hummel.   

UBS lowered its EPS forecast for Stellantis (STLA) in 2025 and 2026 by 50%, and warned the auto stock is not attractive to investors looking for cash returns in the foreseeable future. 

Shares of Stellantis (STLA) were up 1.5% in premarket trading on Monday. The auto stock is down more than 30% on a year-to-date basis.
